channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Follow NPR's annotated fact check of President Trump's address to Congress
item.description: As President Donald Trump addresses a joint session of Congress tonight, reporters from across NPR's newsroom will fact check and offer context as the speech unfolds.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Veterans fired from federal jobs say they feel betrayed, including some who voted for Trump
item.description: Veterans who have been abruptly fired from federal jobs say they feel betrayed by the Trump administration's dramatic downsizing of government.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk comes to Capitol Hill to meet with Republicans who discuss turning DOGE cuts into law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Billionaire Elon Musk arrived on Capitol Hill Tuesday and learned about something new — budget rescissions, an obscure legislative tool that could bring legal heft to his federal budget slashing effort and enshrine the cuts into law.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court allows removal of watchdog agency head as legal battle rages over Trump firing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court in Washington removed the head of a federal watchdog agency on Wednesday in the latest twist in a legal fight over Republican President Donald Trump's authority to fire the special counsel. A three-judge panel on the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's premier and the American president: Two leaders, two speeches, two differing world visions
item.description: Two major speeches were delivered by two of the world’s most powerful leaders this week, on opposite sides of the planet.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans need to cut Medicaid to hit budget savings target, CBO finds
item.description: Republicans can’t achieve their goal of slashing $2 trillion in federal spending over the next decade without cutting Medicaid, according to the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office (CBO).
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: House Speaker Johnson's top aide is arrested on charge of driving under the influence, police say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The chief of staff to House Speaker Mike Johnson was arrested Tuesday night on suspicion of driving under the influence after crashing into a police vehicle following President Donald Trump’s address to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s drive to boost domestic mining stokes long-running debate near BWCA
item.description: As President Donald Trump lifts “undue burdens” on U.S. mines, the shift in the federal posture could boost long-simmering projects in northern Minnesota. Meanwhile, efforts at the state level to slow mining face dim prospects.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms former Trump defense attorney Todd Blanche as deputy attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Republican-led Senate voted Wednesday to confirm Todd Blanche as deputy attorney general, placing President Donald Trump's former criminal defense attorney in a key Justice Department post at a time of turmoil in the agency.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with nation's frustrating search for nuclear waste storage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday wrestled with whether to restart plans to temporarily store nuclear waste at sites in rural Texas and New Mexico even as some justices worried about safety issues and the lack of progress toward a permanent solution.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Veterans are speaking out on the Trump administration's plans to cut the VA's budget
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Stephen Watson served in the Marines for 22 years and receives care through the Department of Veterans Affairs for a traumatic brain injury. He supports President Donald Trump and adviser Elon Musk's cost-cutting program — even if it affects the VA.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 6,000 USDA workers fired by Trump ordered back to work for now
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture must temporarily reinstate nearly 6,000 probationary employees fired since Feb. 13, according to a ruling by the Merit Systems Protection Board.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Trump says no one has heard of Lesotho. But Musk is trying to do business in the African nation
item.description: MASERU, Lesotho (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump says no one has ever heard of Lesotho. The foreign minister of the small African kingdom tells The Associated Press that Trump should “speak for himself.
item.dc:creator: KEKETSO PHAKELA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Kabul airport bombing during Afghanistan pullout has been captured and is due in US court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A suspected senior planner in the suicide bombing at the Kabul airport that killed 13 American service members and roughly 170 Afghan civilians during the chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan has been captured and will appear Wednesday in federal court in the United States to face c
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia Democrat Lucy McBath says she's exploring a 2026 run for governor
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia U.S. Rep. Lucy McBath filed paperwork on Wednesday to raise money for a run for governor in 2026, becoming the first Democrat to take that step. McBath described her effort as an exploratory committee, although that could quickly become a full-blown campaign.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Diego Congress member pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Rep. Sylvester Turner, first-term Democrat, dies at 70
item.description: Rep. Sylvester Turner (D-Texas), the former Houston mayor who won a seat in Congress in November, succeeding the late Rep. Sheila Jackson Lee (D), died Tuesday night. He was 70. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) confirmed Turner’s death Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota farmers fret about ‘chaos and uncertainty’ following latest Trump tariffs
item.description: Minnesota producers say they’re worried about the impact that tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico coupled with retaliatory tariffs could have on the agriculture industry.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Democrats behavior presented party as 'hateful': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly says the behavior from the Democrats was 'hateful' as they did not acknowledge things presented by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump addresses joint session of Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president's speech to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong. But that wasn’t President Donald Trump’s plan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump praises major government cutbacks, efficiency measures
item.description: The president singled out specific spending programs, including an $8 million program promoting LGBTQI+ initiatives in Lesotho.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: How the Trump administration is affecting the U.S. Refugee Admissions Program
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the U.S. Refugee Admissions Program and Minnesota’s history of refugee resettlement.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
